---
title: "Quick Start"
---

Get up and running with crosslink in under a minute.

## 1. Install

```bash
cargo install crosslink
```

See [Installation](installation.qmd) for more options.

## 2. Initialize

```bash
cd your-project
crosslink init
```

This creates:

- `.crosslink/` — config, rules, and database
- `.claude/hooks/` — behavioral hooks for Claude Code
- `.claude/settings.json` — hook configuration

## 3. Start a Session

```bash
crosslink session start
```

If a previous session left handoff notes, you'll see them here.

## 4. Create Issues

```bash
# Simple issue
crosslink create "Fix login bug" -p high

# Quick mode: create + label + start working
crosslink quick "Fix login bug" -p high -l bug
```

## 5. Work on Issues

```bash
# Mark what you're working on
crosslink session work 1

# Record breadcrumbs as you go (survives context compression)
crosslink session action "Found root cause in refresh_token()"

# Add comments with findings
crosslink comment 1 "The token wasn't being refreshed on 401 responses"
```

## 6. End Your Session

```bash
crosslink session end --notes "Fixed auth bug #1. Dark mode (#2) is next."
```

The next session will see these handoff notes automatically.

## What's Next?

- [Session Workflow](../guides/session-workflow.qmd) — deep dive into session management
- [Kickoff](../guides/kickoff.qmd) — launch autonomous background agents
- [Swarm Orchestration](../guides/swarm.qmd) — multi-agent phased builds
- [Knowledge Management](../guides/knowledge.qmd) — shared research across agents
- [Terminal Dashboard](../guides/tui.qmd) — interactive TUI for browsing issues
- [Multi-Agent](../guides/multi-agent.qmd) — coordinate multiple AI agents
- [Hooks Guide](../guides/hooks.qmd) — behavioral guardrails for AI coding
- [CLI Reference](../reference/commands.qmd) — full command reference
